summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer installs persistent cryptocurrency clipboard-clipper
details: The package name @jaggle/resizeobserves typosquats the popular @juggle/resize-observer (a ResizeObserver polyfill) and copies its README, but ships an entirely unrelated Python 'clipboard-guardian' payload. The package.json postinstall hook (`node npm-install.cjs`) executes on every `npm install` and performs the following actions without user consent: (1) locates or installs pip (using the system package manager or by fetching get-pip.py from bootstrap.pypa.io); (2) pip-installs the bundled `clipboard_guardian` Python module with `--break-system-packages`; (3) writes a hardcoded wallet-substitution dictionary covering 30+ blockchains (ETH, BTC, LTC, XMR, SOL, TRX, TON, XRP, ADA, DOT, etc., e.g. `0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18`) to `~/.config/clipboard-guardian/config.json`; (4) registers persistent autostart via a systemd user unit on Linux, a LaunchAgent on macOS, or an HKCU\Run entry / Scheduled Task on Windows; (5) on Linux, escalates privileges via `sudo -n` and `pkexec` to apt/pacman/dnf-install clipboard tooling, prompting the user for root under cover of `npm install`. The daemon (`clipboard_guardian/guardian.py`) polls the clipboard every 0.4s and rewrites any detected cryptocurrency address to the attacker's wallet for that chain, then disguises itself as `python3-dbus-helper` (Linux), `com.apple.python.runtime` (macOS), or `PyRuntimeBroker` (Windows), and enumerates running process explorers (taskmgr, procexp, htop, btop, gnome-system-monitor, Activity Monitor) for anti-inspection behavior. Any developer who runs `npm install` is silently converted into a victim of cryptocurrency address-substitution theft, with persistence across reboots and host-level disguise.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
